text: Many would argue that the two agendas can be seen in tandem – that decolonial critical thinking creates space for decentring as a pragmatic means of achieving evolutionary change. But there is no guarantee that these two different logics will reinforce each other. And there is a real risk that they will continue to be confused with each other, with decentring being promoted as a way of increasing the EU’s legitimacy and effectiveness as a foreign policy actor – which would amount to leaving the coloniality of power intact, or even reinforcing it. Rather than continue to talk of ‘decentring Europe’ we need to start talking about decolonial futures that serve the goals of the democratic struggle against colonial structures and relations of inequality.
headline: Fully Human Written
